title: Psalms 115
subtitle: Not to Us, LORD, Not to Us

sections.header: Verses 1–8 — “Their Idols Are Silver and Gold”

heb.paragraph: "Not to us (lōʾ lānū), LORD, not to us but to your name be the glory" (v.1). The glory-deflection psalm: NOT US, but YOU. Then the idol-critique: they have mouths but cannot speak, eyes but cannot see, ears but cannot hear, noses but cannot smell, hands but cannot feel, feet but cannot walk (vv.4–7). "Those who make them will be like them, and so will all who trust in them" (v.8). You become what you worship.

notes.note: "Not to us, LORD, not to us but to your name be the glory." The double denial: not us, NOT us. All glory directed away from self toward God.

"Their idols are silver and gold, made by human hands. They have mouths but cannot speak. Those who make them will be like them." The most sustained idol-critique in the Psalter. The final line is the theology: you become what you worship. Worship idols, become senseless.

sections.header: Verses 9–18 — “We Will Extol the LORD Both Now and Forevermore”

heb.paragraph: "All you Israelites, trust (bāṭaḥ) in the LORD — he is their help and shield" (v.9). Three groups summoned to trust: Israel (v.9), house of Aaron (v.10), those who fear the LORD (v.11). God remembers and will bless (v.12). "The highest heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth he has given to mankind" (v.16). The living praise God; the dead do not (v.17). "We will extol the LORD, both now and forevermore. Praise the LORD" (v.18).

notes.note: "Trust in the LORD — he is their help and shield." Three groups, one command, one identity: help and shield. Repeated three times for Israel, Aaron, and all who fear.
"The highest heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth he has given to mankind." The divine-human allocation: heaven is God’s; earth is humanity’s stewardship. Dominion (Gen 1:28) restated.
"It is not the dead who praise the LORD. But it is we who extol the LORD, both now and forevermore." The living have the privilege and responsibility of praise. Hallelujah.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: All glory directed away from self toward God…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVNot to us, Lord, not to us but to your name be the glory, because of your love and faithfulness.ESVNot to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness!NIVWhy do the nations say, “Where is their God?”ESVWhy should the nations say, \u201cWhere is their God?\u201d

rec.text: ""Not to us, LORD, not to us but to your name be the glory." The double denial: not us, NOT us. All glory directed away from self toward God."

rows.label: Trust and Praise — 115:9–18
rows.text: Three groups trust. "The earth he has given to mankind." "We will extol the LORD forevermore."

hebtext.note: "Not to us (lōʾ lānū), LORD, not to us but to your name be the glory" (v.1). The glory-deflection psalm: NOT US, but YOU. Then the idol-critique: they have mouths but cannot speak, eyes but cannot see, ears but cannot hear, noses but cannot smell, hands but cannot feel, feet but cannot walk (vv.4–7). "Those who make them will be like them, and so will all who trust in them" (v.8). You become what you worship.

"All you Israelites, trust (bāṭaḥ) in the LORD — he is their help and shield" (v.9). Three groups summoned to trust: Israel (v.9), house of Aaron (v.10), those who fear the LORD (v.11). God remembers and will bless (v.12). "The highest heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth he has given to mankind" (v.16). The living praise God; the dead do not (v.17). "We will extol the LORD, both now and forevermore. Praise the LORD" (v.18).

themes.note: Worship peaks: glory deflected to God. Holiness: you become what you worship. The idol-critique psalm.
